<!-- Generated by the deob-runtime-bringup-design workflow, 2026-06-01. -->

# Deob client — runtime bring-up plan

## Status

Running the compiled deobfuscated RSC client takes four things: (1) a clean compile of the full `reference/rsc-client/src/` tree under **JDK 17** (JDK 21+/26 removed `java.applet`, which `GameShell` extends); (2) a display, satisfied by **Xvfb** (`/usr/bin/Xvfb`, verified) since this box is headless; (3) local game assets, available as the rev-235 content packs in `rscplus/assets/content/`; and (4) a reachable login server with its RSA modulus overridden into the client. The boot machinery itself already EXISTS and is self-contained — `Mudclient.main()` (`Mudclient.java:544`) → `GameShell.startApplication(...)` (`GameShell.java:576`) → `GameFrame` + `LoaderThread` + game thread (`run()`, `GameShell.java:272`) — so no AppletStub launcher layer is needed. **The single biggest blocker is M0: the tree does not yet compile.** `Mudclient.java` is mid-reconciliation (~100 errors under JDK 17, all confined to Mudclient's unresolved obf short names like `da`/`ja`/`lb`), and the 67 subsystem classes are co-dependent with it (11 back-references such as `SocketFactory.equipSlotJk`, `Timer.ze`, `World.Jk`, plus renderer instance callbacks `SurfaceSprite.client.drawPlayer/drawNpc`). Until the concurrent Mudclient-reconcile workflow lands, **nothing on the ladder below can be exercised** — every milestone is gated on M0.

## Milestone ladder

### M0 — Tree compiles to bytecode (prerequisite, currently UNMET)
- **Needed:** reconciled `Mudclient.java` + full 68-file tree compiling under JDK 17 (`/usr/lib/jvm/java-17-openjdk/bin/javac`). There is no build system in `reference/rsc-client/`; compile with `find src -name '*.java' | javac -d out @-`.
- **EXISTS:** all 67 subsystem classes compile in isolation once their Mudclient back-refs resolve; leaf files (e.g. `data/CachePath.java`) compile clean today.
- **ADD:** nothing here — this is the separate Mudclient-reconcile workflow's deliverable (`docs/build/findings/01-deob-compilability.md`, ~1,535 obf-name call sites; `MILESTONE_1_RESULTS.md` is the authoritative compile-state record).
- **Gating dependency:** none above it; **gates M1–M5.**

### M1 — Boots + opens window
- **Needed:** M0 + Xvfb display + JDK 17. Command form:
  `Xvfb :99 -screen 0 1024x768x24 & DISPLAY=:99 /usr/lib/jvm/java-17-openjdk/bin/java -cp out client.Mudclient 0 live`
- **EXISTS:** `Mudclient.main()` (`Mudclient.java:544`) parses `args[0]`=nodeid / `args[1]`=mode / `args[2..]`=`members`/`veterans`, sets `client.isApplet=false`, and calls `client.a(false, "local.runescape.com", 32+prio, "classic", nodeId+7000, (byte)112, displayDepth, 512, 346)` — the obf name for `startApplication(...)`, which builds the `GameFrame` (`GameFrame.java:150`, pure AWT), wires `LoaderThread` (daemon, prio 10; the Win32 cache-file block at `LoaderThread.java:417` is skipped on OpenJDK), and starts the game thread.
- **ADD:** the host/cache config (M2's ADDs) so `loadJagex` (`GameShell.java:619`) does not NPE; under Xvfb `GraphicsEnvironment.isHeadless()` flips false and `Frame.getGraphics()`/`createImage()` succeed (verified). **Naming-drift fix:** `main()` references `GameShell.audioQueue`, `GameShell.fontMetrics`, `super.startLoaderThread` and calls `client.a(...)`, but the committed `GameShell.java` declares `startApplication`/`startApplet` with no such fields — these are reconciled as part of M0.
- **Gating dependency:** M0.

### M2 — Title screen + cache
- **Needed:** M1 + staged local content reachable by the client.
- **EXISTS:** the live asset path is HTTP content packs, **not** a Jagex disk cache. Every game-data load goes through `StreamBase.loadResource(-101, name, pct, contentIndex)` (`StreamBase.java:224`), building URL `BASE_URL + "content" + idx + "_" + hex(Buffer._junkArray[idx])`; the CRC table is fetched by `CacheUpdater.downloadAndVerifyCrcs` from `BASE_URL + "contentcrcs" + hex(millis)` (`CacheUpdater.java:173`). On OpenJDK the Win32 disk-cache and `globalArchive` are null, so the archive machinery is bypassed.
- **ADD:** (a) point `BASE_URL` at a static file host serving `rscplus/assets/content/` (which contains exactly `content0_229aa476 … content11_7d5437c5` + `contentcrcs`, the matching rev-235 set — confirmed); OR pre-stage and suppress the HTTP update by passing `doUpdate <= 20` (`GameShell.java:598` — note `main()` currently passes `(byte)112`, so the update IS attempted against `http://127.0.0.1:port`). (b) Wire `serverHost`, since standalone `getCodeBase()` returns null and `loadGameConfig` (`Mudclient.java:1194-1209`) otherwise NPEs. (c) **Package the boot fonts/logo:** `loadJagex` reads `jagex.jag` (logo.tga) + 8 bitmap fonts as a `.jag` archive via `StreamBase.loadResource`/`readDataFile`; `rscplus/assets/jf/` has the bare `.jf` fonts but **no `jagex.jag` is present in assets** (confirmed) — it must be assembled into the archive form or that boot path adapted. Content index **0** (GameData → `SocketFactory.initGameData`) and **8** (2d graphics) are the minimum to reach `drawLoginScreen`.
- **Gating dependency:** M1.

### M3 — Connects + logs in
- **Needed:** M2 + a running OpenRSC server + RSA-modulus override + a provisioned account.
- **EXISTS:** the full connect/login path. `loginUser(int,String,String,boolean)` (`Mudclient.java:1797`) connects via `createSocket(...,serverHost)` → `ClientStream`, builds opcode-0 LOGIN (marker 10, four session-key words, username, padding), RSA-encrypts with `rsaBlock.rsaEncrypt(BitBuffer.RSA_MODULUS, -118, FontBuilder.rsaPublicExponent)` (`Mudclient.java:1888`) using raw `modPow` (`Buffer.java:620`, matches OpenRSC `Crypto.decryptRSA`), then XTEA-encrypts the tail and seeds ISAAC. Constants (golden-ratio `0x9E3779B9`, 32 XTEA rounds) match OpenRSC byte-for-byte.
- **ADD:** override `BitBuffer.RSA_MODULUS` + `FontBuilder.rsaPublicExponent` to the server's key (see ADD list); set `serverHost`/port to the live server; reuse the cradle's provisioned account.
- **Gating dependency:** M2 + server up.
- **Risk to confirm (not a key issue):** the deob's classic block puts **username in the RSA block, password+UID in the XTEA tail**, whereas the cradle's `proto/v235/login.go BuildRSABlock` (the >204 layout) puts **password in RSA, username in XTEA**. Confirm OpenRSC's `LoginPacketHandler` accepts the deob's classic ordering; if it diverges, adjust `loginUser`'s block construction (not the RSA key). Version gap is tolerated: deob `ClientIOException.BUILD_REVISION = 234` vs server 235, and OpenRSC `enforce_custom_client_version: false`.

### M4 — Live world frame
- **Needed:** M3 + complete content matched to the server revision so `loadRegion`/`drawWorld` succeed (content **9** models, **11** textures, **6**/**4** landscape+map, **1** entity sprites — all in `rscplus/assets/content/`).
- **EXISTS:** the render pipeline (`SurfaceSprite`, `Scene`, `World`).
- **ADD:** nothing new beyond full asset staging.
- **Gating dependency:** M3. **Caveat:** renderer pixel-equivalence is unproven — 4 known drift sites at `scene/GameModel.java:302/304/346`.

### M5 — Playable
- **Needed:** M4 + input loop + full opcode-handler correctness (unmeasured).
- **Gating dependency:** M4.

## Concrete ADD list (small units of work)

### ADD-1 — Standalone `main()` / launcher overrides
The entry already EXISTS (`Mudclient.main`, `Mudclient.java:544`); we only inject config before it drives `startApplication`. Cleanest is a tiny wrapper or a few lines at the top of `main()`:

```java
public static void main(String[] args) {
    // 1. RSA: point at the local OpenRSC server key (see ADD-2).
    BitBuffer.RSA_MODULUS = new BigInteger(
        "7634250561283973106419144827843935010165327069935723928109242614288318739395804201883596278169185387687268116837066108754542364007806573724086207095863517");
    FontBuilder.rsaPublicExponent = new BigInteger("65537");

    // 2. Host/port: standalone getCodeBase() is null, so pin directly (see ADD-3).
    //    serverHost + port written before loginUser connects (Mudclient.java:1839).

    // 3. Content host: BASE_URL -> local static file server over rscplus/assets/content,
    //    OR pre-stage cache and pass doUpdate <= 20 to skip the HTTP CacheUpdater.

    // 4. Existing boot: Mudclient client = new Mudclient(); client.isApplet = false;
    //    client.a(false, "local.runescape.com", 32+prio, "classic", port,
    //             (byte)20 /* <=20 to skip update */, displayDepth, 512, 346);
}
```

### ADD-2 — RSA modulus/exponent override
Exact fields (both already `public static`, directly assignable — no bytecode patching, unlike rscplus's `JConfig`/`JClassPatcher`):
- `client.net.BitBuffer.RSA_MODULUS` (`BitBuffer.java:50`) — currently the Jagex 1024-bit `ca950472…`; set to the OpenRSC modulus.
- `client.ui.FontBuilder.rsaPublicExponent` (`FontBuilder.java:92`) — `new BigInteger("10001",16)` = 65537; matches OpenRSC, set explicitly for clarity.

Recommend adding a one-line setter for cleanliness:
```java
public static void setServerRsa(BigInteger modulus, BigInteger exponent) {
    BitBuffer.RSA_MODULUS = modulus;
    FontBuilder.rsaPublicExponent = exponent;
}
```
**The modulus is environment-specific** — OpenRSC's `Crypto.generateRSAKeys` self-generates a 512-bit keypair on first boot into `client.pem`/`server.pem` (`openrsc/server/.../Crypto.java:94-124`). The value above is the one captured in `proto/v235/rsa.go:25`; if the server's `.pem` was regenerated, read the live modulus from `client.pem` or the server startup log (`Crypto.getPublicModulus()`, `Server.java:498`) before baking it.

### ADD-3 — Host/port config
Standalone `getCodeBase()` is null, so `loadGameConfig` (`Mudclient.java:1194-1209`) cannot derive the host. Set `serverHost` and the login port directly before `loginUser` connects (`Mudclient.java:1839` → `StreamFactory.createSocketFactory(port, host)` → `SocketFactory.host`/`port`, `SocketFactory.java:109,129`). Target the local OpenRSC: **host `localhost`, port `43596`** (the "openrsc" world the cradle uses, `cmd/cradle/main.go:77`; `default.conf:21` binds 43594 for the default world — pick one and match its key).

### ADD-4 — Local-cache config
Serve `rscplus/assets/content/` (verified: `content0..11` + `contentcrcs`, the 13 big-endian CRCs matching the filename hex suffixes one-for-one) via a static HTTP host at `BASE_URL`, OR pre-stage and pass `doUpdate <= 20` to skip the `CacheUpdater` HTTP pull (`GameShell.java:598`). **Boot fonts/logo:** assemble `jagex.jag` (logo.tga + the 8 `.jf` fonts from `rscplus/assets/jf/`) into the `.jag` archive form `loadJagex` expects (`EntityDef.extractArchiveEntry` name-hash `hash*61+(ch-' ')` + BZip2, `EntityDef.java:285`) — **the `jagex.jag` archive itself is not in assets and must be built or its boot path adapted** (confirmed absent). Also ensure `CachePath.initialize` runs (writes `~/.jagex_cache_<slot>/`, writable here).

### ADD-5 — Xvfb (no headless harness needed for the windowed path)
Use Xvfb for the full windowed `GameShell`/`GameFrame` loop — verified to flip `isHeadless()` false and make `Frame.getGraphics()` work under JDK 17. A pure-headless harness (no Xvfb) is only viable to drive the render subsystem in isolation (`BufferedImage.getGraphics()` works headless; `SurfaceSprite` renders into its own `int[]`, mirroring the reflection approach in `rscplus/dumprender/PatchAndRender.java:70-102`) — not the applet run loop, which calls `getGraphics()` on a `Frame`.

## Server story

- **Reachable server:** the same OpenRSC instance the cradle uses — world "openrsc" on **`localhost:43596`** (`cmd/cradle/main.go:77`; per-world map in `openrsc/server/default.conf:21`, default world = 43594). It speaks the rev-234/235 protocol the deob targets and tolerates the 1-rev gap (`enforce_custom_client_version: false`).
- **Server is NOT currently running or built.** OpenRSC source is present (`openrsc/server`, `build.gradle`, libs in `lib/`) but there is no server jar and nothing listens on 4359x. **It must be built + started before M3.**
- **RSA:** override `BitBuffer.RSA_MODULUS` to the live server modulus (the captured value in `proto/v235/rsa.go:25` is correct only for the instance that generated it; read `client.pem` after boot if regenerated). Exponent 65537 matches both sides.
- **Credentials:** reuse the cradle's already-provisioned account — username (cradle default `alex`) + password via the `WESTWORLD_PASSWORD` env var (`cmd/cradle/main.go:124`). No in-deob account creation needed.
- **Offline fallback if no server:** M1→M2 (boot + title screen) are fully reachable **without** any server — the assets are local and login is not required to reach `drawLoginScreen`. Beyond that, only the **headless render harness** (ADD-5) can exercise the renderer offline against the local content packs (no login). M3+ require the live server.

## Execution checklist

Gating note: **steps 1–4 are blocked on M0 (Mudclient compile) landing.** Steps 0a–0c are server/asset prep that can proceed in parallel today.

0a. **Prep — build the server.** Build OpenRSC (`openrsc/server`, `build.gradle`), start world "openrsc" on `localhost:43596`, and capture the modulus it writes to `client.pem` (or the `Crypto.getPublicModulus()` startup log). Provision/confirm an account (`alex` + `WESTWORLD_PASSWORD`). *(Parallelizable now.)*

0b. **Prep — stage assets.** Confirm `rscplus/assets/content/` (`content0..11` + `contentcrcs`) and `rscplus/assets/jf/` are in place (done). **Build the `jagex.jag` boot archive** (logo.tga + 8 `.jf` fonts) or adapt `loadJagex`. Stand up a static file host for the content dir, or plan to pre-stage + skip the update. *(Parallelizable now.)*

0c. **Prep — pin the toolchain.** Confirm `/usr/lib/jvm/java-17-openjdk/bin/{javac,java}` (done) and `/usr/bin/Xvfb` (done). Never use the default JDK 26.

1. **[GATED on M0] Compile.** When Mudclient reconciliation lands: `cd reference/rsc-client && find src -name '*.java' | /usr/lib/jvm/java-17-openjdk/bin/javac -d out @-`. Expect 0 errors. (Until then this fails with ~100 Mudclient errors / 11 subsystem back-refs.)

2. **[GATED on M0] Apply the ADDs.** Inject ADD-2 (RSA override, using the modulus from step 0a), ADD-3 (host=`localhost`, port=`43596`), and ADD-4 (point `BASE_URL`/cache at staged content; pass `doUpdate <= 20`) into `main()`/launcher.

3. **[GATED on M0] M1 — boot.** `Xvfb :99 -screen 0 1024x768x24 &` then `DISPLAY=:99 /usr/lib/jvm/java-17-openjdk/bin/java -cp out client.Mudclient 0 live`. Confirm the `GameFrame` opens and `loadJagex` loads logo+fonts without NPE.

4. **[GATED on M0] M2 — title screen.** Confirm content index 0 (GameData) + 8 (2d graphics) load and `drawLoginScreen` renders. (Reachable with server still down.)

5. **[GATED on M0 + server up] M3 — login.** With the OpenRSC server running and RSA overridden, log in as `alex`. If login fails, verify OpenRSC's `LoginPacketHandler` accepts the deob's classic block ordering (username-in-RSA / password-in-XTEA) vs the >204 layout in `proto/v235/login.go`; adjust `loginUser` block construction if they diverge.

6. **[GATED on M3] M4 — live frame.** Stage full content (9/11/6/4/1); confirm `loadRegion`/`drawWorld` render a scene. Watch the known renderer drift sites (`scene/GameModel.java:302/304/346`).

7. **[GATED on M4] M5 — playable.** Exercise the input loop and opcode handlers; validate interactive play.
